concepts.title: Human Sperm Fertilizes the Egg in the Ampulla
concepts.description: Human sperm fertilizes the egg in the ampulla of the fallopian tube, which is the widest part of the tube.

concepts.title: Structure of Sperm
concepts.description: The sperm consists of head, neck, mid-piece, and tail. The head contains the nucleus with the genetic material. The neck connects the head to the mid-piece. The mid-piece contains the mitochondria, which provide energy for the movement of the sperm. The tail provides the propulsion for the movement of the sperm.

concepts.title: Role of Ovaries in Menstrual Cycle
concepts.description: Ovaries produce and release eggs (oocytes) during ovulation, which is regulated by the menstrual cycle. The menstrual cycle has two phases: the follicular phase and the luteal phase. During the follicular phase, the follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) causes the growth of follicles in the ovaries. The mature follicle ruptures, releasing an egg into the fallopian tube.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for Menstrual Cycle Phases
concepts.description: The menstrual cycle has two phases: the follicular phase and the luteal phase. To remember the order, use the mnemonic: 'Feel Lovely, Get Pregnant'. F - Follicular (Feel), L - Luteal (Lovely), G - Get (Get Pregnant).

concepts.title: Confusion Between Menstrual Cycle and Menstruation
concepts.description: Students often confuse the menstrual cycle with menstruation. The menstrual cycle is the series of changes that prepare the body for a possible pregnancy, while menstruation is the shedding of the uterine lining when there is no pregnancy. To avoid this mistake, remember that the menstrual cycle is the broader term, while menstruation is a part of the cycle.

concepts.title: Time of Ovulation and Fertilization
concepts.description: Ovulation occurs 14 days before the start of the next menstrual period, and fertilization usually occurs within 24 hours of ovulation.

concepts.category: One of: 'Most Important Fact', 'NCERT Line', 'Board Favourite', 'Memory Trick', 'Common Mistake', 'Units to remember'
concepts.title: Role of Uterus in Pregnancy
concepts.description: The uterus is the organ that supports the growth and development of the fertilized egg during pregnancy. It expands to accommodate the growing embryo, and by the end of pregnancy, it holds the fully formed fetus.

concepts.title: Process of Implantation
concepts.description: Implantation is the process by which the fertilized egg attaches itself to the lining of the uterus. It occurs 6-10 days after ovulation and is crucial for the establishment of pregnancy.
